dark-plus" style="background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E;color:#1f2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4" language="json"><code language="json" numberOfLines="5"><span class="line"><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">{</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">  &quot;version&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;0.0.1&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">  &quot;autoVerify&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#569CD6">false</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">  &quot;configurations&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: [</span><span style="color:#82071E;--shiki-light-font-style:italic;--shiki-dark:#F44747;--shiki-dark-font-style:inherit">...</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">}</span></span>
</code></pre></div></div><div class="print:hidden" data-fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true" style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E"></div></div>
<span data-as="p">When disabled, preview tools are still available and you can ask Claude to verify at any time. Auto-verify makes it automatic after every edit.</span>
<h4 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="configuration-fields"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#configuration-fields" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Configuration fields</span></h4>
<span data-as="p">Each entry in the <code>configurations</code> array accepts the following fields:</span>
<div data-table-wrapper="true" class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"><div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table"><table class="m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&amp;_td]:min-w-[150px] [&amp;_th]:text-left [&amp;_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums"><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><code>name</code></td><td>string</td><td>A unique identifier for this server</td></tr><tr><td><code>runtimeExecutable</code></td><td>string</td><td>The command to run, such as <code>npm</code>, <code>yarn</code>, or <code>node</code></td></tr><tr><td><code>runtimeArgs</code></td><td>string[]</td><td>Arguments passed to <code>runtimeExecutable</code>, such as <code>[&quot;run&quot;, &quot;dev&quot;]</code></td></tr><tr><td><code>port</code></td><td>number</td><td>The port your server listens on. Defaults to 3000</td></tr><tr><td><code>cwd</code></td><td>string</td><td>Working directory relative to your project root. Defaults to the project root. Use <code>${workspaceFolder}</code> to reference the project root explicitly</td></tr><tr><td><code>env</code></td><td>object</td><td>Additional environment variables as key-value pairs, such as <code>{ &quot;NODE_ENV&quot;: &quot;development&quot; }</code>. Don’t put secrets here since this file is committed to your repo. Secrets set in your shell profile are inherited automatically.</td></tr><tr><td><code>autoPort</code></td><td>boolean</td><td>How to handle port conflicts. See below</td></tr><tr><td><code>program</code></td><td>string</td><td>A script to run with <code>node</code>. See <a href="#when-to-use-program-vs-runtimeexecutable" class="link">when to use <code>program</code> vs <code>runtimeExecutable</code></a></td></tr><tr><td><code>args</code></td><td>string[]</td><td>Arguments passed to <code>program</code>. Only used when <code>program</code> is set</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div>
<h5>When to use <code>program</code> vs <code>runtimeExecutable</code></h5>
<span data-as="p">Use <code>runtimeExecutable</code> with <code>runtimeArgs</code> to start a dev server through a package manager. For example, <code>&quot;runtimeExecutable&quot;: &quot;npm&quot;</code> with <code>&quot;runtimeArgs&quot;: [&quot;run&quot;, &quot;dev&quot;]</code> runs <code>npm run dev</code>.</span>
<span data-as="p">Use <code>program</code> when you have a standalone script you want to run with <code>node</code> directly. For example, <code>&quot;program&quot;: &quot;server.js&quot;</code> runs <code>node server.js</code>. Pass additional flags with <code>args</code>.</span>
<h4 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="port-conflicts"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#port-conflicts" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Port conflicts</span></h4>
<span data-as="p">The <code>autoPort</code> field controls what happens when your preferred port is already in use:</span>
<ul>
<li><strong><code>true</code></strong>: Claude finds and uses a free port automatically. Suitable for most dev servers.</li>
<li><strong><code>false</code></strong>: Claude fails with an error. Use this when your server must use a specific port, such as for OAuth callbacks or CORS allowlists.</li>
<li><strong>Not set (default)</strong>: Claude asks whether the server needs that exact port, then saves your answer.</li>
</ul>
<span data-as="p">When Claude picks a different port, it passes the assigned port to your server via the <code>PORT</code> environment variable.</span>
<h4 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="examples"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#examples" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Examples</span></h4>
<span data-as="p">These configurations show common setups for different project types:</span>

<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;runtimeArgs&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: [</span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;run&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">, </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;dev&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">],</span></span>

<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;port&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#B5CEA8">3000</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;autoPort&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#569CD6">true</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">    },</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">    {</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;name&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;api&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;runtimeExecutable&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;npm&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;runtimeArgs&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: [</span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;run&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">, </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;start&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">],</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;cwd&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;server&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;port&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#B5CEA8">8080</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;env&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: { </span><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">&quot;NODE_ENV&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;development&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4"> },</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;autoPort&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#569CD6">false</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">    }</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">  ]</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">}</span></span>
</code></pre></div></div><div class="print:hidden" data-fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true" style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E"></div></div></div><div dark-plus" style="background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E;color:#1f2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4" language="json"><code language="json" numberOfLines="11"><span class="line"><span <span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">  &quot;version&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;0.0.1&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>

<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;program&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0A3069;--shiki-dark:#CE9178">&quot;server.js&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">,</span></span>

<span class="line"><span style="color:#116329;--shiki-dark:#9CDCFE">      &quot;port&quot;</span><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">: </span><span style="color:#0550AE;--shiki-dark:#B5CEA8">4000</span></span>
<span class="line"><span style="color:#1F2328;--shiki-dark:#D4D4D4">    }</span></span>

</code></pre></div></div><div class="print:hidden" data-fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true" style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E"></div></div></div></div></div>
<h2 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="schedule-recurring-tasks"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#schedule-recurring-tasks" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Schedule recurring tasks</span></h2>
<span data-as="p">Scheduled tasks start a new local session automatically at a time and frequency you choose. Use them for recurring work like daily code reviews, dependency update checks, or morning briefings that pull from your calendar and inbox.</span>
<span data-as="p">Tasks run on your machine, so the desktop app must be open and your computer awake for them to fire. See <a href="#how-scheduled-tasks-run" class="link">How scheduled tasks run</a> for details on missed runs and catch-up behavior.</span>
<div class="callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-blue-200 bg-blue-50 dark:border-blue-900 dark:bg-blue-600/20" data-callout-type="note"><div class="mt-0.5 w-4" data-component-part="callout-icon"><svg width="14" height="14" viewBox="0 0 14 14" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="size-4 text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300" aria-label="Note"><path fill-rule="evenodd" clip-rule="evenodd" d="M7 1.3C10.14 1.3 12.7 3.86 12.7 7C12.7 10.14 10.14 12.7 7 12.7C5.48908 12.6974 4.0408 12.096 2.97241 11.0276C1.90403 9.9592 1.30264 8.51092 1.3 7C1.3 3.86 3.86 1.3 7 1.3ZM7 0C3.14 0 0 3.14 0 7C0 10.86 3.14 14 7 14C10.86 14 14 10.86 14 7C14 3.14 10.86 0 7 0ZM8 3H6V8H8V3ZM8 9H6V11H8V9Z"></path></svg></div><div class="text-sm prose dark:prose-invert min-w-0 w-full [&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-light dark:[&amp;_kbd]:bg-background-dark [&amp;_code]:!text-current [&amp;_kbd]:!text-current [&amp;_a]:!text-current [&amp;_a]:border-current [&amp;_strong]:!text-current text-blue-800 dark:text-blue-300" data-component-part="callout-content"><span data-as="p">By default, scheduled tasks run against whatever state your working directory is in, including uncommitted changes. Enable the worktree toggle in the prompt input to give each run its own isolated Git worktree, the same way <a href="#work-in-parallel-with-sessions" class="link">parallel sessions</a> work.</span></div></div>
<span data-as="p">To create a scheduled task, click <strong>Schedule</strong> in the sidebar, then <strong>+ New task</strong>. Configure these fields:</span>
<div data-table-wrapper="true" class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"><div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table"><table class="m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&amp;_td]:min-w-[150px] [&amp;_th]:text-left [&amp;_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums"><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td>Name</td><td>Identifier for the task. Converted to lowercase kebab-case and used as the folder name on disk. Must be unique across your tasks.</td></tr><tr><td>Description</td><td>Short summary shown in the task list.</td></tr><tr><td>Prompt</td><td>The instructions sent to Claude when the task runs. Write this the same way you’d write any message in the prompt box. The prompt input also includes controls for model, permission mode, working folder, and worktree.</td></tr><tr><td>Frequency</td><td>How often the task runs. See <a href="#frequency-options" class="link">frequency options</a> below.</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div>
<span data-as="p">You can also create a task by describing what you want in any session. For example, “set up a daily code review that runs every morning at 9am.”</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="frequency-options"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#frequency-options" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Frequency options</span></h3>
<ul>
<li><strong>Manual</strong>: no schedule, only runs when you click <strong>Run now</strong>. Useful for saving a prompt you trigger on demand</li>
<li><strong>Hourly</strong>: runs every hour. Each task gets a fixed offset of up to 10 minutes from the top of the hour to stagger API traffic</li>
<li><strong>Daily</strong>: shows a time picker, defaults to 9:00 AM local time</li>
<li><strong>Weekdays</strong>: same as Daily but skips Saturday and Sunday</li>
<li><strong>Weekly</strong>: shows a time picker and a day picker</li>
</ul>
<span data-as="p">For intervals the picker doesn’t offer (every 15 minutes, first of each month, etc.), ask Claude in any Desktop session to set the schedule. Use plain language; for example, “schedule a task to run all the tests every 6 hours.”</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="how-scheduled-tasks-run"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#how-scheduled-tasks-run" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">How scheduled tasks run</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Scheduled tasks run locally on your machine. Desktop checks the schedule every minute while the app is open and starts a fresh session when a task is due, independent of any manual sessions you have open. Each task gets a fixed delay of up to 10 minutes after the scheduled time to stagger API traffic. The delay is deterministic: the same task always starts at the same offset.</span>
<span data-as="p">When a task fires, you get a desktop notification and a new session appears under a <strong>Scheduled</strong> section in the sidebar. Open it to see what Claude did, review changes, or respond to permission prompts. The session works like any other: Claude can edit files, run commands, create commits, and open pull requests.</span>
<span data-as="p">Tasks only run while the desktop app is running and your computer is awake. If your computer sleeps through a scheduled time, the run is skipped. To prevent idle-sleep, enable <strong>Keep computer awake</strong> in Settings under <strong>Desktop app → General</strong>. Closing the laptop lid still puts it to sleep.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="missed-runs"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#missed-runs" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Missed runs</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">When the app starts or your computer wakes, Desktop checks whether each task missed any runs in the last seven days. If it did, Desktop starts exactly one catch-up run for the most recently missed time and discards anything older. A daily task that missed six days runs once on wake. Desktop shows a notification when a catch-up run starts.</span>
<span data-as="p">Keep this in mind when writing prompts. A task scheduled for 9am might run at 11pm if your computer was asleep all day. If timing matters, add guardrails to the prompt itself, for example: “Only review today’s commits. If it’s after 5pm, skip the review and just post a summary of what was missed.”</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="permissions-for-scheduled-tasks"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#permissions-for-scheduled-tasks" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Permissions for scheduled tasks</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Each task has its own permission mode, which you set when creating or editing the task. Allow rules from <code>~/.claude/settings.json</code> also apply to scheduled task sessions. If a task runs in Ask mode and needs to run a tool it doesn’t have permission for, the run stalls until you approve it. The session stays open in the sidebar so you can answer later.</span>
<span data-as="p">To avoid stalls, click <strong>Run now</strong> after creating a task, watch for permission prompts, and select “always allow” for each one. Future runs of that task auto-approve the same tools without prompting. You can review and revoke these approvals from the task’s detail page.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="manage-scheduled-tasks"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#manage-scheduled-tasks" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Manage scheduled tasks</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Click a task in the <strong>Schedule</strong> list to open its detail page. From here you can:</span>
<ul>
<li><strong>Run now</strong>: start the task immediately without waiting for the next scheduled time</li>
<li><strong>Toggle repeats</strong>: pause or resume scheduled runs without deleting the task</li>
<li><strong>Edit</strong>: change the prompt, frequency, folder, or other settings</li>
<li><strong>Review history</strong>: see every past run, including ones that were skipped because your computer was asleep</li>
<li><strong>Review allowed permissions</strong>: see and revoke saved tool approvals for this task from the <strong>Always allowed</strong> panel</li>
<li><strong>Delete</strong>: remove the task and archive all sessions it created</li>
</ul>
<span data-as="p">You can also manage tasks by asking Claude in any Desktop session. For example, “pause my dependency-audit task”, “delete the standup-prep task”, or “show me my scheduled tasks.”</span>
<span data-as="p">To edit a task’s prompt on disk, open <code>~/.claude/scheduled-tasks/&lt;task-name&gt;/SKILL.md</code> (or under <a class="link" href="/docs/en/env-vars"><code>CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR</code></a> if set). The file uses YAML frontmatter for <code>name</code> and <code>description</code>, with the prompt as the body. Changes take effect on the next run. Schedule, folder, model, and enabled state are not in this file: change them through the Edit form or ask Claude.</span>
<h2 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="environment-configuration"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#environment-configuration" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Environment configuration</span></h2>
<span data-as="p">The environment you pick when <a href="#start-a-session" class="link">starting a session</a> determines where Claude executes and how you connect:</span>
<ul>
<li><strong>Local</strong>: runs on your machine with direct access to your files</li>
<li><strong>Remote</strong>: runs on Anthropic’s cloud infrastructure. Sessions continue even if you close the app.</li>
<li><strong>SSH</strong>: runs on a remote machine you connect to over SSH, such as your own servers, cloud VMs, or dev containers</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="local-sessions"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#local-sessions" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Local sessions</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Local sessions inherit environment variables from your shell. If you need additional variables, set them in your shell profile, such as <code>~/.zshrc</code> or <code>~/.bashrc</code>, and restart the desktop app. See <a class="link" href="/docs/en/env-vars">environment variables</a> for the full list of supported variables.</span>
<span data-as="p"><a class="link" href="/docs/en/common-workflows#use-extended-thinking-thinking-mode">Extended thinking</a> is enabled by default, which improves performance on complex reasoning tasks but uses additional tokens. To disable thinking entirely, set <code>MAX_THINKING_TOKENS=0</code> in your shell profile. On Opus, <code>MAX_THINKING_TOKENS</code> is ignored except for <code>0</code> because adaptive reasoning controls thinking depth instead.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="remote-sessions"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#remote-sessions" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Remote sessions</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Remote sessions continue in the background even if you close the app. Usage counts toward your <a class="link" href="/docs/en/costs">subscription plan limits</a> with no separate compute charges.</span>
<span data-as="p">You can create custom cloud environments with different network access levels and environment variables. Select the environment dropdown when starting a remote session and choose <strong>Add environment</strong>. See <a class="link" href="/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web#cloud-environment">cloud environments</a> for details on configuring network access and environment variables.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="ssh-sessions"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#ssh-sessions" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">SSH sessions</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">SSH sessions let you run Claude Code on a remote machine while using the desktop app as your interface. This is useful for working with codebases that live on cloud VMs, dev containers, or servers with specific hardware or dependencies.</span>
<span data-as="p">To add an SSH connection, click the environment dropdown before starting a session and select <strong>+ Add SSH connection</strong>. The dialog asks for:</span>
<ul>
<li><strong>Name</strong>: a friendly label for this connection</li>
<li><strong>SSH Host</strong>: <code>user@hostname</code> or a host defined in <code>~/.ssh/config</code></li>
<li><strong>SSH Port</strong>: defaults to 22 if left empty, or uses the port from your SSH config</li>
<li><strong>Identity File</strong>: path to your private key, such as <code>~/.ssh/id_rsa</code>. Leave empty to use the default key or your SSH config.</li>
</ul>
<span data-as="p">Once added, the connection appears in the environment dropdown. Select it to start a session on that machine. Claude runs on the remote machine with access to its files and tools.</span>
<span data-as="p">Claude Code must be installed on the remote machine. Once connected, SSH sessions support permission modes, connectors, plugins, and MCP servers.</span>
<h2 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="enterprise-configuration"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#enterprise-configuration" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Enterprise configuration</span></h2>
<span data-as="p">Organizations on Teams or Enterprise plans can manage desktop app behavior through admin console controls, managed settings files, and device management policies.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="admin-console-controls"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#admin-console-controls" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Admin console controls</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">These settings are configured through the <a href="https://claude.ai/admin-settings/claude-code" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">admin settings console</a>:</span>
<ul>
<li><strong>Enable or disable the Code tab</strong>: control whether users in your organization can access Claude Code in the desktop app</li>
<li><strong>Disable Bypass permissions mode</strong>: prevent users in your organization from enabling bypass permissions mode</li>
<li><strong>Disable Claude Code on the web</strong>: enable or disable remote sessions for your organization</li>
</ul>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="managed-settings"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#managed-settings" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Managed settings</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Managed settings override project and user settings and apply when Desktop spawns CLI sessions. You can set these keys in your organization’s <a class="link" href="/docs/en/settings#settings-precedence">managed settings</a> file or push them remotely through the admin console.</span>
<div data-table-wrapper="true" class="[--page-padding:20px] overflow-x-auto flex w-[calc(100%+(var(--page-padding)*2))] my-[1em] py-[1em] -mx-[var(--page-padding)] max-w-none [contain:inline-size]"><div class="px-[var(--page-padding)] grow max-w-none table"><table class="m-0 min-w-full w-full max-w-none table [&amp;_td]:min-w-[150px] [&amp;_th]:text-left [&amp;_td[data-numeric]]:tabular-nums"><thead><tr><th>Key</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><code>disableBypassPermissionsMode</code></td><td>set to <code>&quot;disable&quot;</code> to prevent users from enabling Bypass permissions mode. See <a class="link" href="/docs/en/permissions#managed-only-settings">managed settings</a>.</td></tr></tbody></table></div></div>
<span data-as="p">For the complete list of managed-only settings including <code>allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly</code> and <code>allowManagedHooksOnly</code>, see <a class="link" href="/docs/en/permissions#managed-only-settings">managed-only settings</a>.</span>
<span data-as="p">Remote managed settings uploaded through the admin console currently apply to CLI and IDE sessions only. For Desktop-specific restrictions, use the admin console controls above.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="device-management-policies"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#device-management-policies" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Device management policies</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">IT teams can manage the desktop app through MDM on macOS or group policy on Windows. Available policies include enabling or disabling the Claude Code feature, controlling auto-updates, and setting a custom deployment URL.</span>
<ul>
<li><strong>macOS</strong>: configure via <code>com.anthropic.Claude</code> preference domain using tools like Jamf or Kandji</li>
<li><strong>Windows</strong>: configure via registry at <code>SOFTWARE\Policies\Claude</code></li>
</ul>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="authentication-and-sso"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#authentication-and-sso" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Authentication and SSO</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Enterprise organizations can require SSO for all users. See <a class="link" href="/docs/en/authentication">authentication</a> for plan-level details and <a href="https://support.claude.com/en/articles/13132885-setting-up-single-sign-on-sso" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">Setting up SSO</a> for SAML and OIDC configuration.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="data-handling"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#data-handling" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Data handling</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Claude Code processes your code locally in local sessions or on Anthropic’s cloud infrastructure in remote sessions. Conversations and code context are sent to Anthropic’s API for processing. See <a class="link" href="/docs/en/data-usage">data handling</a> for details on data retention, privacy, and compliance.</span>
<h3 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="deployment"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#deployment" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Deployment</span></h3>
<span data-as="p">Desktop can be distributed through enterprise deployment tools:</span>
<ul>
<li><strong>macOS</strong>: distribute via MDM such as Jamf or Kandji using the <code>.dmg</code> installer</li>
<li><strong>Windows</strong>: deploy via MSIX package or <code>.exe</code> installer. See <a href="https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12622703-deploy-claude-desktop-for-windows" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">Deploy Claude Desktop for Windows</a> for enterprise deployment options including silent installation</li>
</ul>
<span data-as="p">For network configuration such as proxy settings, firewall allowlisting, and LLM gateways, see <a class="link" href="/docs/en/network-config">network configuration</a>.</span>
<span data-as="p">For the full enterprise configuration reference, see the <a href="https://support.claude.com/en/articles/12622667-enterprise-configuration" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" class="link">enterprise configuration guide</a>.</span>
<h2 class="flex whitespace-pre-wrap group font-semibold" id="coming-from-the-cli"><div class="absolute" tabindex="-1"><a href="#coming-from-the-cli" class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link" aria-label="Navigate to header">​<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="gray" height="12px" viewBox="0 0 576 512"><path d="M0 256C0 167.6 71.6 96 160 96h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C98.1 144 48 194.1 48 256s50.1 112 112 112h72c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H160C71.6 416 0 344.4 0 256zm576 0c0 88.4-71.6 160-160 160H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c61.9 0 112-50.1 112-112s-50.1-112-112-112H344c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24h72c88.4 0 160 71.6 160 160zM184 232H392c13.3 0 24 10.7 24 24s-10.7 24-24 24H184c-13.3 0-24-10.7-24-24s10.7-24 24-24z"></path></svg></div></a></div><span class="cursor-pointer">Coming from the CLI?</span></h2>
<span data-as="p">If you already use the Claude Code CLI, Desktop runs the same underlying engine with a graphical interface. You can run both simultaneously on the same machine, even on the same project. Each maintains separate session history, but they share configuration and project memory via CLAUDE.md files.</span>
<span data-as="p">To move a CLI session into Desktop, run <code>/desktop</code> in the terminal. Claude saves your session and opens it in the desktop app, then exits the CLI. This command is available on macOS and Windows only.</span>
<div class="callout my-4 px-5 py-4 overflow-hidden rounded-2xl flex gap-3 border border-green-200 bg-green-50 dark:border-green-900 dark:bg-green-600/20 [&amp;_[data-component-part=&#x27;callout-icon&#x27;]]:mt-px" data-callout-type="tip"><div class="mt-0.5 w-4" data-component-part="callout-icon"><svg width="11" height="14" viewBox="0 0 11 14" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="text-green-800 dark:text-green-300 w-3.5 h-auto" aria-label="Tip"><path d="M3.12794 12.4232C3.12794 12.5954 3.1776 12.7634 3.27244
